intro: Mitrasacme squamigera is a perennial herb native to Australia, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Mitrasacme squamigera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat coughs, colds, and respiratory infections.
